pages.rules:
# League Rules and Policy

Last updated 1/5/2026.

---

## Part 1. League Rules

### Field Directions

Field directions can be found under the Fields tab.

https://www.islandfastpitch.com/fields

### Bases

All teams are required to have a set of bases to bring to all scheduled games.

### Make-up Games

Games cancelled by weather will be rescheduled within 48 hours. These games will be posted to the schedule available online.

### Schedules

Schedules are subject to change at any time. Please check your schedule often for any changes and make-ups that may have been added.

Any dates that your team is unavailable to play must be given at the time of registration. We will do our best to accommodate each team.

Please be aware that schedules are final at the time of release. We will not be making mid season changes.

### Warm Ups

The field will be open 30 minutes prior to game time for warm-up in the outfield only.

### Official Ball

| Divisions | Ball |
| --- | --- |
| 12U, 14U, 16U, 18U, 18+ | 12" official USSSA game ball |
| 10U | 11" official USSSA game ball |

### Injuries

Any injuries occurring during the game at the field must be submitted to our office in a written report.

Please be aware that if you do not purchase team insurance, the league and the field owner are not held responsible in any way.

### Player Suspension

Island Fastpitch holds the right to suspend a player, manager, or team depending on the offense at any given time during your season. Reasons may include, but are not limited to, use of profanity, unsportsmanlike behavior, false information given on registration forms, etc.

Should a player, manager, or coach be ejected from a game, he or she must leave the field of play immediately or risk the chance of his or her team forfeiting the game.

Umpires also have the authority to remove anyone from any of the grounds you are scheduled to play at.

### Ejection Rule

Any player, parent, or coach ejected from a game will be ineligible as indicated.

**1st Offense.** The player or coach is ineligible for the next immediate game.

- Example: if you are ejected out of game one from a doubleheader, you are ineligible for game 2 but will be eligible for your next scheduled game.
- If the ejected player or coach fails to sit the next game, a forfeit win will be awarded to the opposing team. The player or coach will be required to sit out his or her next game, and the team representative or manager will also sit out his or her next game for failure to comply.
- If the ejection occurs in the final game of the season, the penalty will carry over to the playoffs or next season.

**2nd Offense.** Any player or coach ejected a second time will be declared ineligible for the remainder of the season, including playoffs and tournaments.

**Note.** In addition to the above suspensions, further disciplinary action may be taken against an ejected player or coach.

**Appeal Process.** A twenty dollar ($20) money order payable to Island Softball must be included with your written appeal. The fee will be refunded if the ejection is overturned.

Parents that are unable to conduct themselves in a respectable manner may be asked to leave the field. If a problem persists, the parent may be asked not to return to the field. The umpire onsite, along with the owner or director, have the ability to make such decisions.

We are looking to create a friendly, competitive atmosphere for all players, coaches, and parents.

### Rosters

- The roster freeze date will be released at the start of the season. At this time we need all players' birth certificates on file in order for these players to be eligible for playoffs.
- At no time may there be more than 20 players on your active roster.
- You may add or remove players from your roster up until the freeze date. After the roster is frozen, players may not be added.
- Players must have at least 2 plate appearances to play in the playoffs. This includes walks and sacrifices.

### Standings

Standings will be updated, and scores will be posted, once we receive them from both managers.

### Forfeits

If your team is unable to make a game and must forfeit, please call Kaitlin at 631-455-9258.

A forfeit will result in a 7-0 game loss.

If you forfeit two times in one season, your team will be removed from the roster.

### USSSA Rules

Island Fastpitch follows USSSA rules. You will find links to USSSA information under USSSA in the Information tab.

https://www.islandfastpitch.com/usssa

---

## Part 2. League Play. Game Rules and Guidelines

### Rules that apply to every division

These rules are the same for 10U, 12U, 14U, and 16/18U.

**Rainouts.** Announcements regarding rainouts will be made by Kate via group text.

**Coin Flip.** The umpire will flip a coin to determine home team and away team while discussing ground rules.

**Batters.** A maximum of 11 players can bat. If you want to bat more than 11 players, you must bat all players.

**Runs.** Teams must have 12 runs after the 4th inning and 10 runs after the 5th inning.

**Score Reporting.** Scores must be texted to Kate at 631-455-9258 immediately following the game by both coaches.

**Footwear Policy.** No metal cleats or spikes are allowed. Players must wear turf shoes or plastic cleats.

**Playoffs.**

- 8 team division: the top 4 teams qualify for the playoffs.
- 12 team division: the top 6 teams qualify for the playoffs.
- Player eligibility: a player must have participated in at least two games to qualify for playoffs.
- Rosters must be submitted on the Island Fastpitch website as well as on www.USSSA.com.

### Rules by division

| | 10U | 12U | 14U | 16/18U |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Pitching mound | 35 feet | 40 feet | 43 feet | 43 feet |
| Umpires, weeknight single game | One umpire | Two umpires | Two umpires | Two umpires |
| Umpires, weekend doubleheader | One umpire | Two umpires | Two umpires | Two umpires |
| Umpire fee, single game | $40 per team | $80 per team | $80 per team | $80 per team |
| Umpire fee, doubleheader | $80 per team | $160 per team | $160 per team | $160 per team |
| Weeknight game duration | One two hour game, finish inning | One two hour game, finish inning | One two hour game, finish inning | One two hour game, finish inning |
| Weekend doubleheader duration | 1 hour and 20 minutes, finish inning, per game | 1 hour and 20 minutes, finish inning, per game | 1 hour and 20 minutes, finish inning, per game | 1 hour and 20 minutes, finish inning, per game |
| Third strike rule | A batter is out after their third strike | Not listed, USSSA rules apply | Not listed, USSSA rules apply | Not listed, USSSA rules apply |
| Outfielders | Only three outfielders are allowed, not four | Not listed, USSSA rules apply | Not listed, USSSA rules apply | Not listed, USSSA rules apply |
| Game ball | 11" official USSSA game ball | 12" official USSSA game ball | 12" official USSSA game ball | 12" official USSSA game ball |

### 10U only rules

- **3rd Strike Rule.** A batter is out after their third strike.
- **Outfielders.** Only three outfielders are allowed, not four.
- **Umpires.** One umpire per doubleheader for weekend games, and one umpire per single game for weeknight games.

<!--
FOR LEAGUE REVIEW, NOT PUBLISHED.
The scraped source page ends with an extra block of division rules that carries no division
heading. Its rules match the common rules above, with two differences: the pitching mound is
set at 40 feet, and the game duration reads "1 hour and 20 minutes to finish the inning" with
no separate weeknight and weekend lines. Its umpire fees are $160 per team for a doubleheader
and $80 per team for a single game. The Official Ball rule in Part 1 lists an 18+ division,
which has no rules block of its own on the page, so this may belong to 18+. Confirm with the
league which division this block is for before publishing it.
-->

pages.leagues:
# Leagues

Island Fastpitch runs girls fastpitch leagues on Long Island in three seasons: Fall, Summer and Spring. Fall 2026 is our launch season.

Age divisions are 8U, 10U, 12U, 14U and 16/18U. Summer also offers a College Division.

---

## Fall League

**2026 season begins: TBA.** The start date has not been announced yet.

### Weeknight Leagues

Five game season under the lights.

| Division | Game format |
| --- | --- |
| 12U, 14U, 16/18U | Single 7 inning games, or 2 hours, finish the batter |
| 10U | Single 6 inning games, or 2 hours, finish the batter |

Games start at 6:00 pm. Start times may vary.

Nights by age group:

| Night | Division |
| --- | --- |
| Monday | 10U |
| Tuesday | 12U |
| Wednesday | 16/18U |
| Thursday | 14U |

Make up games are scheduled on available fields on varying days.

Playoffs: the top four teams qualify.

### Weekend Leagues

Saturdays and Sundays. Ten game season.

Divisions: 10U, 12U, 14U, 16/18U.

Games are 1 hour and 20 minutes, finish the inning. Doubleheaders start at either 9:00 am or 12:30 pm, mixed start times.

Playoffs: the top four teams qualify.

### Sunday Night League

Sundays. Start times are 4:00 pm and 7:30 pm, mixed starts.

Divisions: 10U, 12U, 14U, 16/18U.

Playoffs: the top four teams qualify.

### 8U Weekend League

Five game season, one single 6 inning game per week.

Start times are 9:00 am and 1:00 pm, mixed starts.

No playoffs. All 8U players receive medals.

<!-- BUILDER NOTES, not for publication:
     Fall times above are from Adam 7/21/26 and SUPERSEDE the old Wix Fall page.
     1. CONFLICT: weeknight now reads "finish the batter", but content/rules.md says
        "finish inning" under ALL FOUR divisions (copied from his live rules page).
        If finish-the-batter is right, rules.md MUST be edited to match, or the new
        site contradicts itself and coaches will argue it at the field.
     2. CONFLICT: his Fall page said Sunday starts were 9:00 or 1:00; Adam says 12:30.
     3. Sunday Night League was absent from his Fall page entirely; Adam confirmed
        it runs. Its game length is assumed to match the Weekend league, CONFIRM.
     4. College Division in Fall is still unconfirmed. -->

### Fall Coaches Meeting

**Date: TBA. Time: 8:00 pm.**

Island Fastpitch Office
1 Edgewood Avenue, Ste 200
Smithtown

---

## Summer League

Season begins the weekend of June 13th.

Sub leagues offered:

- Summer Sunday Night League
- Summer Weeknight League
- Summer Weekend League
- Summer College Division Weeknight League
- Summer College Division Weekend League
- Summer 8U Weekend League

### Summer Coaches Meeting

Date and time TBA.

Island Fastpitch Office
1 Edgewood Avenue, Ste 200
Smithtown

---

## Spring League

Spring league information is coming for 2027.

Sub leagues offered:

- Spring Sunday Night League
- Spring Weeknight League
- Spring Weekend League
- Spring 8U Weekend League

### Spring Coaches Meeting

Date and time TBA.

Island Fastpitch Office
1 Edgewood Avenue, Ste 200
Smithtown

---

## League Fees

All fees are per team and do not include umpire fees.

| League | Fee |
| --- | --- |
| Weeknight | $795 plus umpire fees |
| Weekend | $795 plus umpire fees |
| College Division | $795 plus umpire fees |
| 8U Weekend | $500 plus umpire fees |

Fees must be paid in full before the season begins.

<!-- BUILDER NOTE, not for publication: the live Wix Fall League page lists Weeknight $895 and Weekend $500, which conflicts with the Spring and Summer pages ($795 / $795 / $500). Confirm the Fall numbers with the league office before this page goes live. -->

### Home Field Advantage Discount

We recognize the significance of a home field advantage, so we offer a **$200 discount on league fees** for teams that use their home field for at least half of their scheduled games.

Conditions:

- Teams must qualify for Home Field Advantage.
- Island Fastpitch will not cover any extra field use fees.
- Exclusions apply. Weeknight league fields must have lights.

Contact us to take advantage of this offer.

### What the League Fee Includes

- Softballs
- Scorebook
- Rosters

Rosters must be on USSSA.

---

## Umpire Fees

Umpires are paid at the field, not to the league.

| Game type | Cost per team |
| --- | --- |
| 1 umpire game | $40 |
| 1 umpire doubleheader | $80 |
| 2 umpire doubleheader | $140 |
| 2 umpire playoff game | $70 |
| 2 umpire championship game | $70 |

### Umpires by Division

| Crew | Divisions |
| --- | --- |
| One umpire | 8U, 10U, 12U |
| Two umpires | 12U, 14U, 16/18U |

Two umpires work all playoff and championship games.
pages.tournaments:
# Tournaments

Island Fastpitch runs a full 2026 lineup of tournaments, with both Open and C Level events across the Spring, Summer, and Fall.

All tournaments use USSSA umpires, so teams get a consistent standard of play and officiating from event to event.

## Levels

**Open events** are played on turf fields. Open events include upgraded MVP awards such as belts, gold chains, and spinner medals.

**C Level events** are played on dirt fields. C Level events include a basic MVP award.

## Awards

Champions and Finalists are both recognized.

- Champions receive a Championship Award, a Banner, Medals, and MVP awards.
- Finalists receive a Finalist Award and Medals.

## Game guarantees

**One day tournaments** guarantee three games. On one day weekends, 10U and 12U divisions play Saturday and 14U and 16U divisions play Sunday.

**Weekend tournaments** guarantee four games for all divisions.

## 2026 Tournament Schedule

| Tournament | Date | Age Divisions | Level | Location | Entry Fee |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

Twenty eight tournaments are listed above. The Fall tournament dates are not yet posted.

### Notes on individual events

**Season Opener.** The site carries two listings for this event. One lists Saturday April 4 with 10U, 12U, 14U, and 16U at Open and C Levels. The other lists April 4 and 5, with 10U, 12U, and 12C on Saturday and 14U, 14C, and 16U on Sunday. Confirm the division schedule when you register.

**Midnight Madness tournaments.** Breaking Dawn 16/18U, Breaking Dawn 14U, The Upside Down 12U, The Upside Down 14U, and Don't Get Left Behind 16/18U are Midnight Madness events at the SUSA Bubble in Central Islip, NY. Each is capped at 6 teams. Start times are 11:00 pm. Games run 1 hour and 5 minutes drop dead, and revert back to the last completed inning.

**Friday Night Lights.** These are one day Friday night tournaments with a three game guarantee. First pitch is 5:30 pm.

**Long Island Championship.** This is the annual summer ending tournament played for rings.

### Host club contacts

Several tournaments are hosted by member clubs. Contact the host directly for details.

| Tournament | Host | Contact |

| Chiefs Fastpitch You Matter! | Chiefs Fastpitch | John Wolff, 516-476-5293 |
| Chiefs Firecracker Classic | Chiefs Fastpitch | John Wolff, 516-476-5293 |

| LI Express Wounded Warrior | Long Island Express | Ken Passante, 631-664-5781 |
| Thunder Summer Storm | Long Island Thunder | Joe Rigano, 516-369-7098 |
| Thunderstorm College Showcase | Long Island Thunder | Joe Rigano, 516-369-7098 |

## Pricing

Island Fastpitch weekend tournaments are priced per team, with C Level entries running below Open.

| Event Type | Open | C Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Weekend tournament, 4 game guarantee | $700 per team. Select weekend events are $600 per team. | $525 per team |
| One day tournament, 3 game guarantee | $500 per team | $425 per team |

Single division events are priced as one flat fee per team, with no separate Open and C Level pricing.

| Event Type | Entry Fee |
| --- | --- |
| Friday Night Lights, one day, 3 game guarantee | $475 per team |
| Midnight Madness, 6 team max | $799 per team |
| Host club tournament, 4 game guarantee | $600 per team |
| Thunderstorm College Showcase | $900 per team |

**Umpire fees are paid at the field.** Umpire fees are not included in the entry fee.
pages.contact:
# Information and Contact

Reference page for coaches, parents and players. Covers how to reach the league, USSSA affiliation, the player waiver, team roster submission, and hotel options for visiting tournament teams.

## Contact Island Fastpitch

| | |
|---|---|
| Contact | Mike Alicea |
| Phone | 631-831-4793 |
| Email | mike.islandusssa@gmail.com |
| Website | islandfastpitch.com |

**Office address**

Island Fastpitch
1 Edgewood Avenue, Suite 200
Smithtown, NY 11787

### Contact form

The website also has a contact form. It asks you to choose one of the following roles, then collects your details and message.

- Choose one: Coach, Parent, or Player
- First name (required)
- Last name (required)
- Email (required)
- Write a message

Social media links are listed on the contact page.

## Information menu

The Information section of the site collects the following:

- Hotel Accommodations
- Player Waiver
- Team Roster
- Rules and Policies
- Player Ads
- USSSA Links

## USSSA

Island Fastpitch is a USSSA affiliated league. USSSA is the United States Specialty Sports Association. The USSSA page links to the following resources:

- USSSA Age Calculator, used to confirm which division age a player belongs in
- USSSA Rule Book
- USSSA registration

Teams playing in Island Fastpitch events register with USSSA, follow the USSSA rule book, and confirm player ages against the USSSA age calculator.

## Player Waiver

Every player must have a waiver on file. The waiver is submitted online. For players under 18, a parent or legal guardian completes and agrees to it.

### What the waiver says

In consideration of being allowed to participate in any way in the United States Specialty Sports Association athletic and sports program, related events and activities, the undersigned acknowledges, appreciates, and agrees that:

1. The risk of injury and/or illness from the activities involved in the program is significant, including the potential for permanent paralysis and death, and while particular rules, equipment, and personal discipline may reduce the risk, the risk of serious injury does exist.
2. The risk to have contact with individuals who have been exposed to and/or have been diagnosed with one or more communicable diseases, including but not limited to COVID-19 or other medical conditions, diseases, or maladies does exist, and it is impossible to eliminate the risk that could be exposed to and/or become infected through contact with or close proximity with an individual with a communicable disease.
3. I knowingly and freely assume all such risk, both known and unknown, even if arising from the negligence of the releases or others, and assume all full responsibility for my participation.
4. I willingly agree to comply with the stated and customary terms and conditions for participation. If, however, I observe any unusual significant hazard during my presence or participation, I will remove myself from participation and bring such to the attention of the nearest official immediately.
5. I, for myself and on behalf of my heirs, assigns, personal representatives and next of kin, hereby release and hold harmless the United States Specialty Sports Association, their officers, officials, agents and/or employees, other participants, sponsoring agencies, sponsors, advertisers, and if applicable, owners and lessers of the premises used to conduct the event ("releases"), with respect to any and all injury, illness, disability, death, or loss or damage to person or property, whether arising from the negligence of the releases or otherwise.
6. I have read this release of liability and assumption of risk agreement, before acknowledging the check box below, fully understand its terms, understand that I have given up substantial rights by agreeing to it on my own behalf or on behalf of the youth participant associated with this guardian account, and I sign it freely and voluntarily without inducement.
7. Photo Release: I give permission for photos to be taken and published.

By acknowledging and agreeing to sign this roster and waiver I agree and verify the following: 1) I consent and agree to assume the risk of participation in these programs; and 2) that I specifically agree to the release as provided herein of all the releases, and, for myself, my heirs, assigns and next of kin, I release and agree to indemnify the releases from any and all liabilities incident to my involvement or participation in these programs even if arising from the negligence of the releases or otherwise.

### What the waiver form collects

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Player first name | Yes | |
| Player last name | Yes | |
| Player date of birth | Yes | Month, day, year |
| Team name | Yes | |
| Parent or legal guardian first name | Yes | Enter N/A if the player is over 18 |
| Parent or legal guardian last name | Yes | Enter N/A if the player is over 18 |
| Parent cell phone | Yes | If the player is under 18 |
| Parent email | Yes | If the player is under 18 |
| Country or region | Yes | |
| Street address | Yes | |
| City | Yes | |
| Zip or postal code | Yes | |
| Agreement to the terms of the Player Waiver | Yes | Checkbox |

## Team Roster submission

The Team Roster page is a submission form for coaches, not a public listing of rosters. A coach fills it out once per team, per division, per season.

### Team and coach information

| Field | Required |
|---|---|
| Team | Yes |
| Division age | Yes |
| Season and year | Yes |
| Coach first name | Yes |
| Coach last name | Yes |
| Coach email | Yes |
| Coach cell phone | Yes |

### Player information

Below the team and coach fields, the form provides 15 player rows. Each row collects:

- Player first and last name
- Player date of birth

Player ages should be checked against the USSSA age calculator before the roster is submitted. Every player on the roster also needs a Player Waiver on file.

## Hotel Accommodations

Thank you for playing an Island Fastpitch Tournament on Long Island. We are delighted to have you with us and want to make your stay as comfortable as possible. Below is a list of nearby hotel options catering to different preferences and budgets. Each accommodation has been chosen for its convenience, comfort, and central proximity to the various fields. While this list is currently limited, we will expand it as we gather additional hotel options. We hope this information makes your planning seamless and your visit enjoyable. Safe travels, and we look forward to seeing you at the tournament.
